findings.recommendation: Rotate the exposed token and remove plaintext sync exports from the workstation.
findings.summary: A staging token was exported into a note-taking workspace sync file.
findings.title: GitHub token pattern detected

findings.recommendation: Move the file into approved storage and reduce local plaintext copies.
findings.summary: A spreadsheet of partner identities is stored outside protected project storage.
findings.title: Plaintext email address collection detected

findings.recommendation: Force password rotation for the affected identity group and require MFA re-enrollment.
findings.summary: finance.ops@example.com appears in multiple synthetic credential leak collections used in the demo.
findings.title: Identity exposure detected in breach intelligence

findings.recommendation: Separate the password set immediately and enable MFA on all internet-facing services.
findings.summary: Three staged business services share the same password candidate.
findings.title: Password reuse detected

findings.recommendation: Replace the short password with a longer passphrase.
findings.summary: A demo account password is materially shorter than the team baseline.
findings.title: Short password detected

findings.recommendation: Restrict the file permissions and remove the backup from shared local storage.
findings.summary: An SSH private key backup is accessible to a broad local group on the workstation.
findings.title: Sensitive file has broad permissions

findings.recommendation: Re-enable browser protection controls and review why they were disabled.
findings.summary: Safe browsing protections are disabled in the primary browser profile.
findings.title: Disabled browser protection setting detected

findings.recommendation: Move credential storage to a managed password manager and clear local browser logins.
findings.summary: Saved business credentials remain available in the primary profile.
findings.title: Saved logins present in browser profile

findings.recommendation: Report the sender, block the message, and warn the targeted finance group.
findings.summary: A staged invoice email uses a mismatched reply address designed to redirect the conversation.
findings.title: Reply-To mismatch detected in email headers

findings.recommendation: Do not visit the URL and block the destination in mail or web controls.
findings.summary: The email links to a fake document portal with urgency language.
findings.title: Suspicious URLs detected in email body

findings.recommendation: Block the destination URL and investigate whether any users interacted with it.
findings.summary: The invoice review URL is present in a malicious URL collection.
findings.title: Threat intelligence match for url

recommended_actions: Block the malicious invoice URL and notify the finance team.
Force-reset reused or breached credentials and verify MFA coverage.
Re-enable disabled browser protections and remove stored business logins.
metadata.risk_explanation: The environment now shows both valid attack vectors and accessible identity material, increasing the likelihood of account takeover and business-email compromise.
metadata.summary: The demo posture has shifted from manageable hygiene gaps into an active identity-risk scenario with phishing, malicious infrastructure, and repeated credential weakness.

suspicious_patterns.summary: The message design and the linked infrastructure point to a staged account-harvesting workflow.
suspicious_patterns.title: Phishing pressure aligns with malicious infrastructure

suspicious_patterns.summary: Breach hits and password reuse indicate that password spray or credential stuffing would likely succeed.
suspicious_patterns.title: Exposed identity set has viable follow-on abuse paths

notes: Demo mode generated synthetic findings. No live scan was executed.
This staged report is designed for dashboard screenshots and walkthroughs.
